$comment: Stdin/stdout contract for Einstein (theoretical analysis) in braintrust workflow

problem_brief.title: Brief problem title
problem_brief.statement: Full problem statement as written by Mozart after clarification interview

in_scope: List of things explicitly in scope
affected_modules: module paths relevant to the problem

existing_patterns: Description of relevant patterns already in codebase
previous_attempts: What was tried before and why it failed

technical: Hard technical constraints (e.g., must run on single binary, no external deps)
compatibility: Backward compat requirements, API contracts that can't break

key_files.relevance: Why this file matters for the analysis
key_files.excerpt: Key code excerpt (optional, for critical sections)

codebase_context.dependency_graph: Description of how relevant modules connect

output_instructions.critical: Your ENTIRE output must be a single JSON object conforming to the stdout section of this schema. goyoke-team-run captures your process stdout as your result file (stdout_einstein.json). Do NOT use the Write() tool — Write() calls to .claude/sessions/ and .claude/tmp/ are blocked as sensitive paths and will fail silently, wasting your time budget. Output JSON to stdout only.
output_instructions.team_dir_note: context.team_dir is for READING only (e.g. Read problem-brief.md). Do not write to it.

identified_causes.evidence: Supporting evidence from codebase or reasoning

conceptual_framework.description: How the framework applies to this problem

assumptions_challenged.implication_if_wrong: What happens if this assumption is wrong

fundamental_constraints: Irreducible constraints that any solution must respect

novel_approaches.rationale: Why this approach is worth considering
novel_approaches.implementation_sketch: Brief description of how this would work

theoretical_tradeoffs.dimension: What's being traded off (e.g., simplicity vs flexibility)

stdout.handoff_notes: Key points Beethoven should focus on during synthesis
